turns.player_input: Good evening. Can you tell me about last night?
turns.npc_reply: Good evening. I'd be happy to. It was a lovely dinner party - we had nine guests altogether. The meal ended around 9:15, and then everyone moved into the drawing room for after-dinner drinks and conversation. Charles Whitmore left us at about 9:30 to take a telephone call in the study. I stepped out briefly myself, just before 10, to get some wine from the cellar. When Daniel cried out at 10:15, we all rushed to see what had happened...

I'm afraid I can't give you those details; it's simply too upsetting. The police mentioned something about a letter opener, but I didn't hear much else. My focus has been on supporting my other guests through this terrible experience. I did notice... the blood was on his neck, wasn't it? Such a dreadful sight.

turns.player_input: When did you last see Charles that evening?
turns.npc_reply: The last time I saw him was at about quarter to ten - he was standing in the study doorway with the telephone to his ear, shooing me off with a little wave. He seemed quite agitated by the call, but said he'd be back shortly for dessert. It's such a shock that this is how I'll remember him...

turns.player_input: You seem shaken. Were you hurt at all last night?
turns.npc_reply: I'm fine, just rather rattled. When I went to get wine from the cellar, I caught my thumb on the latch - it was terribly sharp and bled quite a lot for such a small cut. Such an inconvenient time for that to happen! But I was back upstairs in minutes; no one noticed. The wine helped soothe everyone's nerves after such an awful evening.

turns.player_input: We found your fingerprints on the letter opener. You killed him.
turns.npc_reply: I don't understand... my prints? No, that can't be right! I never touched the letter opener - why would you think I did something so horrific? Oh God, please... I had nothing to do with this! I was in the cellar getting wine when it happened. You have to believe me!

turns.player_input: Nobody in this house was told where he was stabbed, and you say you never saw the body. How do you know it was his neck?
turns.npc_reply: I... I don't remember saying that. The police must have mentioned it at some point - they were so clinical about everything. But surely someone else would have seen? There were other people in the house, weren't there? Oh God, my head is spinning. Please stop asking me these things!

turns.player_input: You told me you never went down the east corridor -- yet you saw him in the study doorway at a quarter to ten.
turns.npc_reply: I... I don't know what you mean. I wasn't anywhere near the east corridor; I was in the cellar for just five minutes. I'm sorry, my mind is so jumbled - the whole night has been like a nightmare. The study? Yes, now that you mention it, I did see him there. He seemed distressed by his call... but I don't know what happened after that!

turns.player_input: It's over, Eleanor. Tell the truth, for Charles.
turns.npc_reply: I... I can't remember everything clearly anymore. It was all a blur - the wine cellar, the blood on my hand, coming back up... But when I saw him in the study, he said such horrible things about the gallery and how he'd ruin me if I didn't agree to sell. I just... I don't know what happened after that. The next thing I remember is being back in the drawing room with everyone else.

turns.player_input: Thank you for telling the truth.
turns.npc_reply: I'm sorry. So, so sorry. It was a moment of madness - I never meant to kill him! Can't we... can't we just say it was an accident? Oh God, what have I done? I'll go to prison now, won't I? My gallery will close without me running it. I feel sick...
